purpose: Classifies equity valuation environments by inflation regime to determine equilibrium P/E ranges and assess current over/undervaluation
components: Regime A (1950-68): P/E 16-18x, low stable inflation, positive equity environment
Regime B (1969-82): P/E 8-12x, high volatile inflation, equity bear market
Regime C (1983-2021): P/E 18-24x, low declining inflation, secular equity bull
Potential Regime D: P/E 15-20x, moderate persistent inflation (2.5-3.5%), if confirmed
Stock-bond correlation regime as mechanism: negative correlation supports higher P/E
Gordon Growth Model decomposition: P/E = 1/(r-star + ERP - g)
current_assessment: Current S&P 500 at ~21-22x forward P/E prices Regime C continuation. If core inflation settles at 2.5-3.5% (Regime D), fair value contracts to 15-20x implying 10-25% overvaluation. Requires 2-3 more years of inflation data to confirm. iter_0023 structural inflation floor of 2.3-2.8% tilts toward Regime D at 6/10 confidence.
limitations: Regime transitions take 2-4 years, providing poor timing guidance
R-star uncertainty (±100-150bp) propagates through the entire framework
Cannot distinguish between AI-productivity-driven higher r-star and deficit-driven higher r-star
